challenges.title: Introduction to Deep Reinforcement Learning
challenges.description: Foundations of RL: agent-environment interaction, policies, rewards, exploration vs exploitation, and applications in game-playing, robotics, and autonomous systems.

challenges.title: Mathematical Foundations and MDPs
challenges.description: Core mathematical concepts: Markov Property, Markov Decision Processes, transition probabilities, reward functions, value functions, Bellman Equations, and dynamic programming methods.

challenges.title: Rewards, Returns and Agent Objective
challenges.description: Reward design, sparse vs dense rewards, reward shaping, intrinsic motivation, curiosity-driven learning, returns and discounting, GAE, n-step returns, and multi-objective RL.

challenges.title: Exploration vs Exploitation
challenges.description: Epsilon-greedy, softmax/Boltzmann, UCB, Thompson Sampling, count-based exploration, curiosity-driven methods, RND, parameter space noise, entropy regularization, and noisy networks.

challenges.title: Evaluating Agents and Value Functions
challenges.description: Policy evaluation with MC and TD methods, eligibility traces, function approximation, GPI, learning curves, benchmarking, statistical significance, sample efficiency, and off-policy evaluation.

challenges.title: Improving Agents and Agent Anatomy
challenges.description: Agent anatomy (policy, value, model), actor-critic architecture, experience replay, target networks, Dyna, MCTS, deadly triad, gradient clipping, normalization, curriculum learning, and distributed RL.

challenges.title: TD Learning and Robust Targets
challenges.description: SARSA, Q-Learning, Expected SARSA, Double Q-Learning, Clipped Double Q-Learning, fixed Q-targets, Double DQN, multi-step learning, PER, distributional RL, and Rainbow DQN.

challenges.title: Value-Based Deep RL and Function Approximation
challenges.description: DQN architecture, CNNs for visual inputs, experience replay, target networks, function approximation, tile coding, RBFs, feature construction, world models, latent space, offline RL, and normalization techniques.

challenges.title: DQN and Stable Value-Based Methods
challenges.description: DQN architecture for Atari, frame preprocessing, stacking and skipping, reward clipping, Double DQN, Dueling DQN, Noisy Nets, C51, Rainbow, and distributed architectures (Ape-X, IMPALA, R2D2).

challenges.title: Sample-Efficient Methods: Dueling DDQN, PER and Distributional RL
challenges.description: Prioritized Experience Replay, sum-tree implementation, importance sampling, Dueling DDQN, QR-DQN, IQN, FQF, data-efficient methods (DER, EfficientZero, Dreamer), and data augmentation (RAD, DrQ, SPR).
